risks: Standard_LRS storage provides no geo-redundancy for the central data lake — a single region failure would cause data loss
Public network access is enabled on all services (ADF, Synapse, Databricks) — exposes the pipeline to internet-facing attack surface
No monitoring or diagnostic resources included (Log Analytics workspace, diagnostic settings) — limits operational visibility into pipeline health and failures
No Key Vault resource for managing secrets and connection strings — credentials may be stored insecurely
Synapse SQL administrator uses SQL authentication (sqladmin) rather than Entra-only authentication — weaker identity posture
Databricks public IP is not disabled (enableNoPublicIp: false) — cluster nodes are reachable from the internet
No resource tagging strategy defined — complicates cost allocation and governance at scale
correctionsRecommended: Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings for ADF, Databricks, and Synapse to enable centralized monitoring
Add an Azure Key Vault resource for managing secrets, connection strings, and SQL admin credentials
Upgrade storage redundancy from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS or Standard_GRS for production disaster recovery
Set Synapse managedVirtualNetwork to 'default' and enable preventDataExfiltration for network isolation
Consider adding private endpoints for Storage, Synapse, and ADF to eliminate public network exposure
Set Databricks enableNoPublicIp to true to prevent public IP assignment on cluster nodes
Add RBAC role assignment resources (Storage Blob Data Contributor) for ADF and Synapse managed identities instead of deferring to post-deployment configuration
Add a resource tagging convention (e.g., environment, costCenter, project) to all resources
